hypothesis: The diffusion model's learned score function encodes information about the curvature of the free energy landscape orthogonal to the reaction coordinate, such that the magnitude of the score difference vector ||s^A_θ(x,t) - s^B_θ(x,t)|| correlates with proximity to the transition state ensemble independent of the interpolation weight κ, enabling unsupervised transition state identification in systems with unknown or poorly defined metastable states.
